claim.content: Let B denote the coefficient of the T^2 term in the low-temperature expansion w(T)=w_0+B T^2 of the electronic thermal resistivity w(T) for CeCoIn₅. The proposition is that B quantifies inelastic electron-electron scattering in the Fermi-liquid sense and scales proportionally to the square of the quasiparticle effective mass entering transport, B ∝ (m^*)^2, so that an observed strong increase of B(H) as a function of applied magnetic field H corresponds to a strong enhancement (divergence-like increase) of the quasiparticle transport mass m^*(H); here m^* denotes the Landau quasiparticle effective mass that governs transport coefficients in Fermi-liquid theory.
